name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America offers payday loans ($100–$500), installment loans ($100–$1,000), and title loans ($2,000–$25,000) with same-day funding in-store and online.
description_long:
Advance America is a nationally recognized consumer lending company that describes itself as fully accredited and serving millions of customers across the United States. The company operates physical store locations as well as an online channel, with bilingual (English and Spanish) staff at many branches. Its Jacksonville location on N. Edgewood Ave. sits in the Commonwealth Village neighborhood, west of downtown, and is open six days a week with extended Friday hours.

The company offers three core loan products. Payday Loans range from $100 to $500 and are structured as two-to-four-week short-term loans designed to bridge gaps between paychecks. Installment Loans range from $100 to $1,000 and allow repayment in multiple payments over time, offering higher amounts and longer terms than payday loans. Title Loans are available online for $2,000 to $25,000 and use the borrower's paid-off vehicle as collateral; these are processed in partnership with LoanCenter, not directly underwritten by Advance America. The store also offers Western Union money transfer services.

Advance America publishes a 4.9-out-of-5 star rating based on 125,419 reviews and claims over 200,000 reviews on Google, suggesting a consistently rated customer service experience. Applicants can pre-qualify by phone before visiting a store, and the company markets same-day access to funds. Spanish-language service is available in-store and prominently advertised. To apply, borrowers need a government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account, and a Social Security Number or ITIN.

Honestly, Advance America's core products—payday and installment loans—are high-cost credit instruments that typically carry triple-digit annual percentage rates. They are designed for short-term emergencies, not ongoing cash flow problems, and can become expensive if rollovers or extensions occur. Title loans carry the additional risk of vehicle repossession if the borrower defaults. Consumers with access to a credit union PAL, employer advance, or 0% intro credit card should exhaust those options first. Advance America is best evaluated as a last-resort option for borrowers with no other short-term credit access.
pros: 4.9/5 star rating across 125,419 verified reviews with 200,000+ Google reviews
Same-day funding available up to $1,000 in-store
Title loans up to $25,000 available online
Bilingual English/Spanish staff — Spanish prominently supported
Phone pre-qualification available before visiting a store
Both in-store and online application channels offered
Western Union money transfers available at store locations
cons: Payday loans capped at $500 — insufficient for most mid-size emergencies
Title loans are processed through third-party LoanCenter, not directly by Advance America
Checking account required — excludes unbanked consumers from payday and installment products
Payday and installment loans carry high APRs typical of short-term consumer lending
Store closed Sundays — limited access on weekends despite emergency-cash positioning
best_for: Employed borrowers needing $500 or less to cover expenses until their next paycheck
Vehicle owners who need $2,000–$25,000 and have a paid-off car to use as collateral
Spanish-speaking consumers who prefer bilingual in-person loan assistance
Consumers who want to walk in without an appointment and leave with same-day cash
diagnosis: Advance America is best for consumers with steady employment who face a short-term cash gap of $1,000 or less and have no access to lower-cost credit. The central caveat is cost: payday and installment loans at this type of lender typically carry very high APRs, and title loans risk vehicle repossession — making these products appropriate only as a genuine last resort.
services: Title Loans ($2,000–$25,000, online, via LoanCenter partnership)
In-store loan processing with same-day funding

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
